name: Applesauce Brownies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. In a bowl, combine oil, applesauce, and cocoa. Mix in sugar until dissolved.
3. Add eggs and vanilla; stir until well combined.
4. In another bowl, mix flour, baking powder, and baking soda.
5. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture and stir until smooth.
6. Grease a 9-inch square pan (or line with wax paper).
7. Sprinkle chopped nuts over the batter.
8. Pour the batter into the prepared pan.
9. Bake for 20-30 minutes, or until the top is set but edges are not dried out. A toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
10. Cut into 16 (or more) squares.
Note: This recipe yields approximately 16 servings, or 24 if using 1/4 cup of sugar per serving as recommended by the American Diabetes Association. The original recipe is similar to a cake in texture.
